title: Generative Models for Sound
concept: The same generative principles (VAEs, GANs, Diffusion) that generate images can be adapted to generate audio waveforms, speech, and music.

exercises.prompt: Which of these have you probably heard made by an AI before?

options.text: An AI-generated song or voice clone
A photo taken with a film camera

exercises.explain: AI can now create realistic music, voices, and sound effects — not just pictures!

cards.title: Can AI Make Music Too?
cards.body: You've seen AI paint pictures and draw faces. But guess what — the exact same brain tricks (VAEs, GANs, Diffusion) can also learn to create sound! Voices, songs, drum beats, even entire albums can come from AI models that never played an instrument.

cards.title: Generative Models for Sound
cards.body: These are AI systems that learn patterns from lots of real audio (like music or speech), then use that knowledge to create brand-new sounds. They turn audio into numbers, learn the patterns in those numbers, and turn new patterns back into sound waves you can hear.

cards.title: Like Learning to Hum a New Tune
cards.body: Imagine hearing thousands of songs, then humming a brand new melody that sounds like it could fit right in — even though you never copied one exact song. That's what these AI models do: they learn the 'feel' of sound and remix it into something new.

cards.title: AI Voices and Songs
cards.body: Apps that clone a person's voice to read a book out loud, or AI tools that write a pop song from scratch — both use these same generative model ideas, just applied to sound waves instead of pixels.

cards.title: Myth: AI Sound Tools Are Totally Different Tech
cards.body: Nope! It's not a separate invention. The very same VAE, GAN, and Diffusion ideas used for images are reused for audio. The only difference is the data type — sound waves instead of pixels.

cards.title: Images vs Sound: Same Idea, Different Data
cards.body: Both use the same generative process — learn patterns, then create something new from them.

left.text: Learns patterns in pixels to create new pictures

right.text: Learns patterns in waveforms to create new audio

exercises.prompt: Which AI model types can be adapted to generate audio, according to this lesson?
exercises.explain: The same core generative model families used for images — VAEs, GANs, and Diffusion — can also learn to create audio.

exercises.prompt: AI models that generate sound need to be built completely differently from AI models that generate images.
exercises.explain: False! They share the same core generative principles — only the type of data (sound vs pixels) changes.

exercises.sentence: Generative models turn audio into numbers, learn the ___ in those numbers, and turn new patterns back into sound.
exercises.explain: AI finds patterns in the numeric representation of sound, then uses those patterns to generate new audio.

exercises.prompt: Spot the AI: Which of these was most likely fully generated by an AI model, never sung or played by a human?

options.text: A brand-new pop song with a voice and melody made entirely by an AI music generator
A recording of a live orchestra concert
A podcast host reading their own script live

exercises.explain: The AI-generated song wasn't performed by any real singer or musician — it was created from learned sound patterns.

exercises.prompt: Why can the same GAN or Diffusion model that draws images also be used to create music or speech?

options.text: Because sound and images are actually the exact same file type
Because the underlying idea — learning patterns from data and generating new data — works no matter what type of data it is
Because computers can only make images, and sound is just a trick
Because musicians secretly record all AI-generated songs

exercises.explain: Generative models are flexible: whether the data is pixels or sound waves, the same core process of learning patterns and generating new examples applies.
